The incredibly massive, deeply humiliating hostage crisis in southern India aggressively continued to paralyze the national security apparatus. On November 16, 1933, the United States and the Soviet Union achieved a massive, historically defining, and deeply strategic diplomatic breakthrough. US President Franklin D. Roosevelt officially and aggressively established formal, massive diplomatic relations with the USSR. This incredibly massive geopolitical maneuver fundamentally ended over a decade of absolute American non-recognition of the deeply entrenched communist state, heavily paving the absolute diplomatic architecture required for the massive, critical "Grand Alliance" that would eventually and violently crush Nazi Germany during World War II.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On November 16, 1846, Pothan Joseph, the incredibly massive, highly influential, and fiercely principled titan of early Indian journalism, was officially born in Kerala. He achieved absolute, historical immortality by heavily serving as the incredibly brilliant, massive founding editor of multiple highly prestigious national newspapers, including *The Hindustan Times* and *Deccan Herald*. His incredibly massive, uncompromising editorial independence and fiercely sharp political commentary heavily shaped the absolute, foundational architecture of modern, massive democratic journalism in the republic.

On November 16, 1914, the incredibly massive, highly strategic, and heavily forceful military action known as the Mesopotamian Campaign aggressively commenced during World War I. The massive British Indian Expeditionary Force D (consisting heavily of incredibly brave Indian sepoys) aggressively landed at Fao in the Persian Gulf. Their incredibly massive, bloody objective was to absolutely secure the incredibly vital oil refineries of Abadan from the Ottoman Empire, heavily ensuring the absolute, massive fuel supply required for the British Royal Navy's global operations.
